---
title: "Data Viz Final Project: Pharmacies in Chicago"
format: html
---

```{python}
# load packages
import geopandas as gpd
import pandas as pd
from pathlib import Path
from shapely import wkt
import pydeck as pdk
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import altair as alt
```

```{python}
# load data
data_path = 'C:\\Users\\blueb\\final_project_AWJP_w26\\data\\raw-data\\Pharmacy_Status_-_Historical_20260302.csv'
df_pharm = pd.read_csv(data_path)
```

```{python}
# explore data
df_pharm.shape
df_pharm.head()
```

```{python}
# check pharmacy status
df_pharm['Status'].unique()

# create mapping for pharmacy status
mapping = {
    'OPEN':'Open',
    'CLOSED': 'Closed',
    'Permanently closed': 'Closed',
    'closed': 'Closed',
    'open': 'Open',
    'permanently closed': 'Closed',
    'unknown': 'Unknown'
}

# recode the status column
df_pharm['Status'] = df_pharm['Status'].replace(mapping)
```

```{python}
# make pharmacy names in all caps
df_pharm['Pharmacy Name'] = df_pharm['Pharmacy Name'].str.upper()
```


```{python}
# load gdf
census_gdf = gpd.read_file('C:\\Users\\blueb\\final_project_AWJP_w26\\data\\raw-data\\Census_Tracts_20260302.geojson')
```

```{python}
# inspect data

# count geometry by type
print('Geometry type:', census_gdf.geom_type)

# count total number of features
print('\n N features:', len(census_gdf))

# get crs
print('\n Coordinate reference system:', census_gdf.crs)
```

```{python}
# create basic visualization
census_gdf.plot().set_axis_off()
```

```{python}
# load pharmacies as a geodataframe

# drop nas
df_pharm = df_pharm.dropna(subset=['New Georeferenced Column'])

# drop na strings
df_pharm = df_pharm[
    df_pharm['New Georeferenced Column']
    .str.lower()
    .ne('nan')
]

# convert wkt to a geometry
df_pharm['geometry'] = gpd.GeoSeries.from_wkt(
    df_pharm['New Georeferenced Column'],
    on_invalid='ignore'
)

# drop failed parses
df_pharm = df_pharm.dropna(subset=['geometry'])

# create geodataframe
pharm_gdf = gpd.GeoDataFrame(
    df_pharm,
    geometry='geometry',
    crs='EPSG:4326'
)

# sanity check
print(df_pharm.shape)
print(df_pharm.head())
```


```{python}
# join gdfs
combined_gdf = gpd.sjoin(pharm_gdf, census_gdf, 
    how='left',
    predicate='within')

# check object type
print('Object type:', type(combined_gdf))

# check geometry type
print('\nGeometry type:', combined_gdf.geom_type.value_counts())
```

```{python}
# other sjoin option


# join gdfs
#combined_gdf = gpd.sjoin(census_gdf, pharm_gdf, 
#    how='left',
#    predicate='intersects')

# check object type
#print('Object type:', type(combined_gdf))

# check geometry type
#print('\nGeometry type:', combined_gdf.geom_type.value_counts())
```


```{python}
combined_gdf.head()
```

```{python}
assert (census_gdf.crs == pharm_gdf.crs)
```

```{python}
# set up plot
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(8,6))

# plot chicago in blue
census_gdf.plot(ax=ax, color='lightblue', alpha=0.5)

# plot pharmacies
pharm_gdf.plot(
    ax=ax,
    column='Status',
    categorical=True,
    legend=True,
    markersize=5,
    alpha=0.7
)
plt.title('Chicago Pharmacy Distribution')
plt.tight_layout()

ax.set_axis_off()
plt.show()
```


```{python}
# group data by census tract
pharm_by_tract = (
    combined_gdf
    .groupby('census_t_1')
    .size()
    .reset_index(name='pharmacy_count')
)

# count pharmacies per census tract
pharm_by_tract.shape
```


```{python}
# count pharmacies per tract
pharm_counts = (
    combined_gdf
    .groupby('tract_ce_3')
    .size()
    .reset_index(name='pharmacy_count')
)
```

```{python}
# merge counts back to census polygons
census_gdf = census_gdf.merge(
    pharm_counts,
    on='tract_ce_3',
    how='left'
)

# fill missing tracts with zero pharmacies
census_gdf['pharmacy_count'] = (
    census_gdf['pharmacy_count']
    .fillna(0)
)
```

```{python}
# project to crs in meters
census_gdf = census_gdf.to_crs(epsg=26916)

# calculate area in square miles
census_gdf['area_sq_miles'] = (
    census_gdf.geometry.area / 2_589_988
)

# compute density
census_gdf['pharm_density'] = (
    census_gdf['pharmacy_count'] /
    census_gdf['area_sq_miles']
)
```
 

```{python}
# make choropleth map
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10,10))

census_gdf.plot(
    column='pharm_density',
    cmap='OrRd',
    scheme='quantiles',
    k=5,
    legend=True,
 #   legend_kwds={'label': 'Pharmacies Per Square Mile'},
    linewidth=0.1,
    edgecolor='grey',
    ax=ax
)

ax.set_title('Pharmacy Density per Square Mile\nChicago Census Tracts')
ax.axis('off')

plt.show()
```

```{python}
census_gdf.columns
```
